description: On a snowy city street during the day, Phoebe and Monica walk side-by-side past a brick building.
Phoebe turns her head toward Monica. "Do you think they have yesterday's Daily News?" she asks. Monica turns slightly and replies, "Why?"
They reach an outdoor newsstand. "I just want to check my horoscope, see if it was right," Phoebe says, looking at the papers.
Monica looks past Phoebe, her eyes widening.
Monica grabs Phoebe's arm and says, "Oh, my God," keeping her gaze fixed ahead.
Phoebe turns her face toward Monica.

Monica leans close to Phoebe. "Phoebe, don't look now, but behind you there's a guy who has the potential to break our hearts and plunge us into a pit of," she says. Coma Guy stands nearby, looking down at a magazine.
Phoebe asks, "Where?" and turns around to look at the man. He continues flipping through the magazine.
Phoebe says, "Ooh, come to mama," and the man begins to turn toward them. Monica quickly pulls Phoebe away, facing forward.
Monica mutters, "Cool. Be cool. Be cool." The man walks past them, turning his head toward Phoebe to say, "Nice hat." Phoebe smiles and replies, "Thanks."
They watch him walk away. "I should do something," Monica says. Phoebe replies, "Whistle."

Monica shakes her head, saying, "I'm not going to whistle." Phoebe says, "Come on, do it." "No," Monica replies. "Do it," Phoebe urges. "No," Monica says. Phoebe leans in and says, "Do it. Do it. Do it. Do."
Phoebe cups her hands around her mouth and yells, "Woohoo!" The man turns and steps backward into the street. An ambulance drives through the frame with screeching tires, completely obscuring him.
Monica and Phoebe gasp, their eyes wide as they raise their gloved hands to their faces. The ambulance remains parked in the street.
The scene goes completely black.
The setting changes to a hospital room where a heart monitor beeps steadily. Coma Guy lies unconscious in a bed as Monica and Phoebe walk in and stand beside him.
Looking at the man, Monica says, "It's all my fault." Phoebe replies, "No, it's mine. Why did I woohoo?"
Monica raises her hands slightly, palms up.
"What was I hoping would happen? That he'd turn around and say, OOH, I love that sound. I must have you," Monica says.
Monica and Phoebe stand at the bedside. On-screen text reads: SUPERVISING PRODUCERS JEFF GREENSTEIN JEFF STRAUSS.
Phoebe looks down at the man and says, "Just wish there was something we could."
Phoebe lowers her face closer to Coma Guy.
"Hello?" Phoebe says softly.
Still leaning over him, Phoebe says, "Hello, coma guy."
Phoebe stares intently at his face.
Phoebe suddenly grabs his shoulders and shakes him. "Get up, you Girl Scout," she shouts, her volume rising sharply.
Phoebe continues shaking him, shouting, "Up." Monica grabs her shoulder and pulls her back, asking, "What are you doing?" Phoebe replies, "Maybe nobody's tried this."
They both look at the unconscious man. On-screen text reads: PRODUCED BY TODD STEVENS.
Monica says, "If we at least knew his name," keeping her gaze lowered.
Phoebe opens her mouth to speak.
On-screen text reads: WRITTEN BY ALEXA JUNGE. Phoebe says, "I know. I hate that they're calling him John Doe. So sad. It's like he's a deer. A female deer." Monica looks at her and says, "Phoebe."
Phoebe turns her head toward Monica.
Phoebe says, "Oh, I was going to stop."
They both look back down at the man in the bed.
Monica says softly, "You."
"Look at that face," Monica adds, looking at the man.

Coma Guy's eyes remain closed. "I mean, even sleeping, he looks smart," Monica says. Phoebe points down toward his hand and says, "Yeah, but did you see the dent in his knuckles? That means he's artistic."
Monica taps her cheek thoughtfully. "Okay? He's a lawyer who teaches sculpting on the side," she says.
Phoebe grins, shifting her shoulders, and says, "He can dance."
On-screen text reads: DIRECTED BY JAMES BURROWS.
Phoebe gestures enthusiastically with her hands. "He's the kind of guy who, when you're talking, he's listening and not saying yeah, I understand," she says.
Phoebe continues, "But really wondering what you look like naked."
Monica nods slowly. They both gaze down at the unconscious man in silence.
"I wish all guys could be like him," Monica says quietly.
Phoebe looks at Monica, her expression still.
"I know," Phoebe says softly.
Phoebe rests her head on Monica's shoulder. The setting changes to the exterior of an apartment building at night, then changes to a living room where Chandler, Joey, and Ross are gathered.
Holding a mug, Ross says, "You."
Ross continues, "I don't get it. You don't even know this guy?" Joey, sitting on the back of the sofa, asks, "Yeah, are there no conscious men in the city for you two?"
Monica opens a box of cereal at the kitchen table while Phoebe stands next to her holding a bowl.
"He doesn't have anyone," Monica says, pouring the cereal. Phoebe adds, "Yeah, we feel kind of responsible." Chandler, standing by the sofa, says, "I can't believe you said woohoo."
Joey leans forward from the back of the sofa.

Joey says, "I don't even say woohoo." Rachel points a remote, and the television screen shows Jay Leno sitting at a desk. Leno says, "She's coming up. Coming up, folks, when we come back, we'll be talking about her new book, Euphoria."
On the television, Jay Leno holds up a book titled "Euphoria Unbound" and says, "Unbound, the always interesting Nora Tyler Bing. You might want to put the kids to bed for this one."
Chandler quickly points the remote at the TV and says, "We don't have to watch this. Weekend at Bernie's is on. Showtime, HBO and Cinemax." The others immediately object, saying, "No way."
Joey turns to Chandler and says, "Come on, she's your mom." Chandler replies, "Exactly."
Chandler gestures with his hand, adding, "Weekend at Bernie's."
Chandler swings his arms in short, chopping motions, saying, "Dead guy getting hit in the groin twenty, thirty times."
Chandler paces away from the television. Rachel approaches him.
Gesturing with both hands, Rachel says, "Chandler, I got to tell you, I love your mom's books. I love her books. I cannot get on a plane without one. I mean, this."

She lowers her hands and finishes, "is so cool." Chandler points a finger at her and says, "Yeah, well, you wouldn't think it's cool if you're eleven years old and all your friends are passing around page 79 of Mistress."
Chandler pauses briefly, maintaining his gaze on her.
Chandler finishes his sentence, saying, "Bitch," clipping the word short.
Ross widens his eyes and says, "Oh, I remember. Page 79."
Monica turns her head quickly and says, "Yow." Joey nods, smiling, and adds, "Oh yeah. The thing after the opera with the girl with a trick hip."
Chandler squeezes his eyes shut and grimaces.
In an apartment living room, Ross, Phoebe, Monica, and Joey sit near a sofa. Chandler stands behind them.
Ross looks over his shoulder and says, "Come on, Chandler, I love your mom. I think she's a blast." Holding a cup and spoon, Chandler replies, "You could say that because she's not your mom."
The door opens and Paolo enters. Rachel greets him, and they kiss. Paolo says, "Please want to say that."
Ross, Joey, and Monica turn their heads to watch the couple kiss. Ross stares with his mouth slightly open.
Ross turns back toward Chandler and asks, "When did Rigatoni get back from Rome?"
Monica, sitting beside Ross, turns to look at him.
Monica answers, "Last night."
Ross gestures with both hands and asks, "Really? So then his plane didn't explode in a big ball of fire?"
Ross clasps his hands together and adds, "Just a dream."
Joey smirks, and Ross looks down, adjusting his posture. Audience laughter plays.
Phoebe, sitting on a patterned chair, sits up quickly and says, "Hey."
Pointing forward, Phoebe continues, "Hey. She's on"
On a television screen, Nora Bing walks onto a talk show set, waving to a cheering audience. Paolo, Chandler, and Rachel look toward the TV.
Pointing at the screen, Paolo says, "Nora Bing"
The group continues watching the TV.
On the television, Jay Leno sits at a desk across from Nora Bing. He asks, "Before we get to the book. Now, what is this about you being arrested in London? What is that all about?"
Phoebe turns her head toward Chandler.
Phoebe says, "Your mom was arrested." Chandler replies, "Busy beaming with pride."
On the television, Nora Bing sits on the set.
Touching her face, Nora Bing says on TV, "This is kind of embarrassing, but occasionally, after I've been intimate with a man"
Chandler, in the apartment, gestures with a spoon and asks, "Now, why would she"
Chandler finishes his sentence, "say that's embarrassing?"
Audience laughter plays from the television.
On TV, Nora Bing says, "I just get this craving for Kung Pao chicken." In the apartment, Chandler frowns and shouts, "That's too much information."
The group continues to watch the TV. Loud audience laughter is audible.
On TV, Nora Bing gestures and says, "Needless to say, he got a huge"
The audience laughter swells. In the apartment, the group claps and cheers, while Chandler stands still.
Chandler touches his neck, staring straight ahead.
On TV, Jay Leno gestures with both hands as he speaks silently, the audience laughter continuing.
Leno continues to speak, gesturing toward Nora Bing, as the laughter fades.
On TV, Nora Bing smiles and says, "Tomorrow, which I hate. But I get to see my son, who I love."
The group in the apartment reacts collectively with a synchronized "Aww."
Gesturing with his hands, Chandler says, "This is the way that I find out. Most moms use the phone."
On TV, Leno looks toward Nora Bing and speaks.
Leno says, "Don't take this wrong. I just don't see you as a mom somehow. I don't mean that."
Placing a hand on her chest, Nora Bing replies, "I don't mean that bad. Oh, no. I am a fabulous"
Nora Bing continues, "mom. I bought my son his first condoms,"
In the apartment, the group immediately turns their heads to stare at Chandler.
Chandler stands holding a notepad and pen, staring back at them. Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler says, "and then he burst into flames."
The group looks away from Chandler. The scene changes to the exterior of a hospital building, then to a heart monitor displaying a rhythm.
A song plays with the lyrics, "Nothing you could make and take me away from my guy." An ambulance passes the hospital building.
Inside a hospital room, Monica reads a newspaper aloud near Coma Guy's bed, saying, "Let's see. Congress is debating a"
Monica continues reading, "new deficit reduction bill. The mayor wants to raise subway fares again."
Monica reads, "The high today was 45." Phoebe arranges flowers in a vase nearby.
Turning a page, Monica reads, "and team Slave board"
Coma Guy lies motionless in the bed. Phoebe smiles.
A song continues with the lyrics, "stick together." A greeting card among the flowers reads, "Love, Phoebe & Monica GET WELL SOON".
The song continues with the lyrics, "I'm telling you,"
The door to the hospital room opens, and Monica walks in.
Phoebe follows Monica into the room, carrying a large potted plant.
The song lyrics, "nothing you could do could make me untrue." play. Sitting by the bed, Monica holds a dark sweater.
Monica drapes the sweater over Coma Guy's chest, tucking it gently around his neck.
The song continues with the lyrics, "Nothing you could buy could make me tell." Sitting by the bed, Phoebe plays an acoustic guitar.
Coma Guy lies with shaving cream on his face. Phoebe applies more cream with a brush.
While applying the shaving cream, Phoebe asks, "What about Glenn?"
Standing beside her, Monica replies, "He could be a Glenn."
Phoebe replies, "Not special enough."
Phoebe continues applying the shaving cream.
Phoebe asks, "Oh. How about Agamemnon?"
Monica shakes her head slightly and replies, "Way too special."
Phoebe keeps brushing the shaving cream onto Coma Guy's face.
A song begins with the lyrics, "This evening," The scene changes to a nighttime view of the New York City skyline.
The cityscape view remains on screen as the segment ends.
In a restaurant, Nora and Chandler sit at a round table with a red tablecloth. Several empty shot glasses are arranged on the table.
Nora looks toward Chandler and says, "I am famished. Do I want?"
Chandler, keeping his gaze on his menu, replies, "Please, God, don't let it be Kung Pao Chicken."
Nora places her arm around Chandler's shoulder.
Nora asks, "How do you watch this show? What did you think?" Chandler replies, "Well, I think you need to come out of your shell. Just"
Chandler lowers his menu. Ross approaches the table, wearing a suit and tie.
Standing behind Nora, Ross says, "What is this dive?"
Ross continues, "Only you could have picked this place." Nora begins to stand up.
Nora turns toward Ross.
Nora approaches Ross, hugs him, and kisses his cheek. She says, "Come on. Gotta have fun. Give me a hug. I heard about the divorce."
Ross says, "Lesbian." Nora replies, "Yeah,"
Ross nods slightly.
Nora touches Ross's arm and says, "Well, you know what they say. Be careful what you wish for." Ross looks downward.
Monica, Phoebe, and Joey are seated at the table holding open menus. Ross moves toward an empty chair next to Chandler.
Nora sits down and says, "I think we're ready for some tequila." Ross takes a seat and adds, "I know I am."
Joey and Phoebe converse, gesturing with their hands. Chandler hands an open menu to Ross.
Ross accepts the menu as Chandler says, "There you go."
Ross looks at the menu and says, "I'm not really a shot drinking kind of guy."
Rachel and Paolo arrive at the table. Rachel says, "Honey. Sorry we're late. We kind of"
Rachel continues, "just lost track of time." Paolo nuzzles Rachel's neck.
Ross lowers his gaze toward his menu with a still expression.
Ross grabs a shot glass from the table, says, "But a man can change," and quickly drinks from it.
Ross places the empty glass down, squeezing his eyes shut and visibly scrunching his face.
Ross pinches the bridge of his nose. Across the table, Paolo chews food.
Ross holds his hand near his eye, creating a circle with his fingers, and says, "Anyone want me to appraise anything?"
Rachel places a piece of food into Paolo's mouth. Ross watches them from his seat.
Rachel kisses Paolo's cheek and strokes his face. Ross rests his chin on his hand, continuing to watch them.
Ross points at the table and says, "Well, I'm going to go to Tinkertown." He begins to stand up.
Ross steps away from the table. Rachel takes a seat next to Monica.
Rachel leans forward and says, "Mrs. Bing, I have to tell you, I have read everything you've ever written. No, I mean it."
Rachel continues, "I mean, when I read Euphoria at Midnight, all I wanted to do was become a writer."
Nora touches her chest and replies, "Oh, please, honey, listen, if I can do it,"
Nora raises her hands, counting on her fingers. "Anybody can. You just start with half a dozen European cities,"
Nora continues gesturing, "throw in 30 euphemisms for male genitalia and bam, you have got"
Nora lowers her hands and finishes, "yourself a book."
Chandler looks toward the others seated at the table.
Chandler gestures toward Nora with an open hand and says, "My mother, ladies and gentlemen."
The setting changes to a hallway. Nora stands holding a payphone receiver to her ear.
Nora says into the phone, "Yeah, any messages for room two? Two six." Ross enters the hallway.
Ross steps further into the hallway, leaning his hand against the wall.
Nora asks, "You okay, slugger?" Ross replies, "Oh, yeah, I'm fine."
Ross stands in front of a closed wooden door. The door suddenly opens, striking him, as an older woman walks out.
Ross stumbles backward from the impact, placing a hand on his chest.
Ross straightens his posture and coughs into his closed hand.
Nora asks, "What is with you tonight?" Ross shakes his head and says, "Nothing."
Ross steps closer to Nora, waving his hands slightly in front of him.
Nora speaks into the phone, "Okay."
Nora says, "Thank you," and hangs up the phone.
Nora crosses her arms and turns to face Ross.
Nora asks, "It's the Italian hand-licker, isn't it?"
Ross replies, "No, it's the one he's licking," placing slight vocal emphasis on the word "licking."
Nora keeps her arms crossed, maintaining eye contact with Ross.
Nora states, "She's supposed to be with you."
Ross shifts his posture slightly.
Ross nods and says, "You're good."
Nora places a hand on her chest.
Nora says, "Oh, Ross, listen to me. I have sold a hundred million copies of my books. And you know why?"
Ross asks, "The girl on the cover with her nipples showing?"
Nora shakes her head, keeping her hands clasped.
Nora unclasps her hands and gestures outward, saying, "No. Because I know how to write men that women fall in love with."
Nora brings her hands closer to her chest and continues, "Believe me, I cannot sell"
Nora gestures with one hand, saying, "Paolo. People will not turn 325 pages for Paolo."
Nora continues, "Come on. The guy's a secondary character. He's just"
Ross watches Nora as she speaks.
Nora points a finger and says, "a complication you eventually kill off."
Ross nods slightly, his gaze fixed on her.
Ross says, "When"
Ross tilts his head slightly as the segment concludes.
In a dimly lit hallway with patterned wallpaper and framed posters, Nora Bing and Ross stand facing each other.
Ross says, "He's not a hero." Nora maintains her gaze on him.
Ross looks slightly downward and then back at Nora.
Nora asks, "You know who our hero is?" Ross replies, "The guy on the cover with his nipples showing?" Nora steps closer, smiling, and says, "No, it's you."
Ross looks away.
Ross says, "Please." Nora steps closer and says, "No, really."
Nora touches Ross's arm and says, "Come on. You're smart."
Nora continues, "You're sexy."
Ross chuckles briefly, looking down, and says, "Right." Nora replies, "Yes, you are. And the fact that you don't think you are makes you even sexier," keeping her gaze on him.
Nora touches his lapel.
Nora pats his chest and says, "Kiddo, you're going to be fine. Believe me."
Nora leans in and kisses Ross on the lips. Ross keeps his eyes open, his arms resting at his sides.
Ross pulls his head back slightly, his eyes wide as he looks at Nora. Nora keeps her hands on his shoulders.
Nora pulls Ross back into another kiss, and they embrace. A door behind them opens, and Joey steps out.
Joey stops and stares at them, then turns and goes back through the door, pulling it shut. Ross and Nora separate, looking toward the door.
Joey opens the door again and stands in the doorway. Ross points toward him and stammers as audience laughter is audible.
Joey gestures slightly and says, "I'll just pee in the street." He steps back inside and shuts the door.
Ross covers his face with one hand, looking down. The scene transitions to the exterior of a corner building on a city street during the day.
The setting changes to an apartment hallway. Joey, wearing a bathrobe, opens an apartment door. Ross stands in the hallway holding a jacket.
Ross asks, "Chandler here?"
Joey says, "Yeah," gesturing inward. Ross grabs Joey's arm, pulls him out into the hallway, and shuts the door.
Ross gestures with his hands and says, "Okay, about last night, you know Chandler? You didn't tell, okay? Because I'm thinking we don't need to tell"
Ross continues, "Chandler. I mean, it was just a kiss, right?"
Ross shrugs and says, "One kiss. No big deal."
Joey looks away, then back to Ross, saying, "Right? Nice. No big deal."
Joey points at Ross and says, "Okay, in Bizarro World," his volume rising.
Joey paces slightly, gesturing with his hand as Ross watches him. Audience laughter is audible.
Joey points at Ross again, saying, "You broke the code."
Ross asks, "What code?" Joey replies loudly, "You don't kiss your friend's mom."
Joey gestures with his hands, saying, "Sisters are okay, maybe a hot looking aunt. But not a mom. Never a mom." Audience laughter is audible.
The apartment door opens. Chandler steps out, picking up a newspaper from the floor. Ross and Joey quickly lean against the wall and doorframe.
Chandler asks, "What are you guys doing out here?" Ross points to Joey and says, "Well, Joey and I had discussed getting in an early morning racquetball game."
Ross points his thumb at Joey and says, "But apparently somebody overslept." Joey smiles and nods.
Chandler turns back towards his apartment door.
Chandler looks at Ross and says, "Yeah, well, you don't have your-" Ross interrupts, saying, "No. No, I don't. Because it's being restrung."
Ross points at Joey.
Ross says, "Somebody was supposed to bring me one." Joey quickly replies, "Yeah, well, you didn't call and leave your grip size."
Chandler looks back and forth between them. Audience laughter is audible.
Chandler says, "You guys spent way too much time together," then turns and walks down the hallway.
Ross and Joey watch Chandler walk away. Ross turns back to Joey.
Ross says, "Okay, I'm scum. I'm scum." Joey throws his hands up and asks, "How could you let this happen?" Ross replies, "Oh, no."
Ross gestures with his hands, saying, "Well, it's not like she's a regular mom."
Ross holds up a fist and says, "She's sexy."
Joey stares at Ross.
Joey steps closer and asks, "You don't think my mom's sexy?"
Ross holds up his hand, his eyes widening. Audience laughter is audible.
Ross says, "Not in the same way."
Joey points to his chest, saying, "Hey, I'll have you know that Gloria Tribbiani was a handsome woman in her day. All right?"
Joey lowers his hand.
Joey asks, "You think it's easy giving birth to seven children?"
Ross shakes his head slightly, keeping his hand raised. Audience laughter is audible.
Ross says, "Okay, I think we're getting into a weird area."
The apartment door behind them opens. A woman with long blonde hair and a man with long dark curly hair step out.
The woman says, "Hey." Joey and Ross look over.
The woman asks, "What are you guys doing out here?" Ross smiles and says, "Well, not playing racquetball."
Joey nods toward Ross.
Joey says, "He forgot to leave his grip size."
Ross points at Joey and says, "He didn't get the goggles."
The woman smiles as audience laughter is audible.
Ross smiles, looking toward the woman. The screen turns black.
